title: Migrate to Grafana Cloud — Self-managed steps

blocks.content: Migrating from a self-hosted Grafana instance to Grafana Cloud traditionally requires extensive API knowledge, custom scripts, and manual configuration of each resource. This process can take hours or days, depending on the complexity of your environment, and is prone to errors that can disrupt monitoring and alerting workflows.

The Grafana Cloud Migration Assistant eliminates these challenges by automating the entire migration process. With a simple UI-guided workflow, you can securely migrate dashboards, data sources, folders, plugins, library panels, and alerting resources to Grafana Cloud in minutes, with real-time progress tracking and automatic validation.

The Grafana Cloud Migration Assistant provides the following advantages over manual migration:

- Eliminate the need for API knowledge or scripting
- Migrate all resources in minutes instead of hours or days
- Securely transfer credentials and sensitive data with encryption
- Preserve resource UIDs and folder hierarchies automatically
- Track migration progress in real-time with detailed status updates
- Reduce errors through automated validation and verification

## Understand migration prerequisites

Successful migrations require proper preparation of both your self-hosted Grafana instance and your Grafana Cloud environment. Taking time to verify these prerequisites prevents migration issues and ensures a smooth transition.

Before starting your migration, verify the following prerequisites:

1. Confirm your source Grafana instance is running version 11.2 or later (v12.0+ for GA)
2. Verify the `onPremToCloudMigrations` feature toggle is enabled (enabled by default in v11.5+)
3. Ensure you have Grafana server administrator access on the source instance
4. Confirm you have Admin role access on your Grafana Cloud stack
5. Verify your source instance has internet access
6. If using a highly-available setup, scale down to one replica
7. Add required IPs and URLs to your network allowlist (if applicable)
Complete the next step in your **Grafana Cloud stack** to generate a migration token. Open the Cloud guide in Pathfinder when you're in your Cloud environment.

## Connect your self-managed instance to Grafana Cloud

Connecting your self-managed instance to Grafana Cloud establishes a secure link between the two environments, enabling resource migration. The migration token authenticates this connection and verifies that both instances can communicate. This connection is read-only from the self-managed instance and won't modify your existing resources.
Sign in to your self-managed Grafana instance as a server administrator.
Navigate to **Home** > **Administration** > **General** > **Migrate to Grafana Cloud**.

Next, you'll click the **Migrate this instance to Cloud** button. A modal will appear where you need to:

1. Paste your migration token into the **Migration token** field
2. Click **Connect to this Stack**
Click the **Migrate this instance to Cloud** button, then paste your token and click **Connect to this Stack** in the modal that appears.
The connection is established and you see the migration assistant interface with options to build a snapshot.

## Build a migration snapshot

A snapshot captures the current state of your selected resources and stores them locally on your instance, ready for upload to Grafana Cloud. The snapshot includes all resource metadata, configurations, and dependencies. When you select a resource that requires other resources, the Migration Assistant automatically includes those dependencies in the snapshot.
Review the available resources for migration displayed in the migration assistant interface.
In Grafana v12.0 and later, you can select specific resources. In versions 11.2-11.6, all supported resources are included automatically.

If using Grafana v12.0 or later, select the checkbox next to each resource type you want to migrate: **Dashboards**, **Folders**, **Data sources**, **App Plugins**, **Panel Plugins**, **Library Panels**, and **Grafana Alerting resources**.

When you select a resource that depends on other resources, those dependencies are automatically included. For example, selecting **Dashboards** automatically includes required **Data sources**, **Plugins**, **Library elements**, and **Folders**.
Click **Build snapshot**.
Wait for the snapshot creation to complete. The interface displays progress as resources are captured.
The snapshot is created and a list of resources appears with Type and Status columns showing 'Not yet uploaded' for each resource.

## Upload resources to Grafana Cloud

Uploading the snapshot securely transfers your resources to Grafana Cloud, where you can track progress in real-time and review any errors that need manual resolution. Resource UIDs are preserved during upload, allowing you to correlate local and cloud resources, and enabling you to re-run migrations to update resources if needed.
Review the snapshot summary showing the total number of resources ready for upload.
Click **Upload snapshot**.

Monitor the real-time progress tracking as resources are migrated. The Status column updates to show resources being uploaded, successfully uploaded resources ('Uploaded to cloud'), and any resources with errors.
Use the column headers to organize the view. Click **Name** to sort alphabetically, **Type** to group by resource type, or **Status** to group by upload status.
Review the updated snapshot information showing total resources, successful migrations, and any errors.
For any resources with errors, expand the error details to understand what requires manual resolution.
The upload completes and you see resources marked as 'Uploaded to cloud' with the snapshot summary showing the number of successfully migrated resources.
Complete the remaining steps in your **Grafana Cloud stack** to verify migrated resources and activate alert rules. Open the Cloud guide in Pathfinder when you're in your Cloud environment.
